_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: How Tracy family, 2009 Rockies offer hope for Red Sox rebuild
items.snark: Columnist digs deep into obscure baseball history to find hope. Brother, we need a lot more than vibes.

items.headline: Red Sox vs Astros preview: Can Boston turn season around at home?
items.snark: Sox at 12-19 hosting the Astros at 12-20. A battle of who wants bottom-feeder status more.

items.headline: Red Sox demote Nate Eaton 24 hours after calling him up from Worcester
items.snark: Eaton got a 24-hour taste of the bigs and never even stepped on the field, living the dream.

items.headline: Ken Rosenthal calls Red Sox coaching overhaul a major disruption
items.snark: If you fire a manager this early you probably shouldn't have brought him back, says man with functioning brain.

items.headline: How do Red Sox boot Brayan Bello from rotation before he does more damage?
items.snark: Bello's getting torched so hard even the bullpen's like 'nah we're good out here.'

items.headline: Yoeilin Cespedes is ablaze: Two-homer night caps wild April for Drive
items.snark: Kid can't stop hitting dingers in Greenville while the big-league lineup takes naps against Canadians.

items.headline: Garrett Crochet confident he'll return from IL in minimum 15 days
items.snark: Crochet's shoulder got fatigued and they IL'd him out of caution, which is code for panic.

items.headline: Alex Cora sends emotional farewell email to Red Sox staff after firing
items.snark: Cora gets one final email before security escorts him out. Still classier than how FSG handled it.

items.headline: Robert Spillane gives promising update after late-season injury woes
items.snark: Spillane's feeling good which means we can all stop holding our breath about linebacker depth.

items.headline: Hunter Henry looking to pay it forward mentoring rookie Eli Raridon
items.snark: Henry teaching Raridon the ancient art of catching passes and not looking like a bust.

items.headline: Patriots deserve an A for this offseason says draft analyst
items.snark: Pats get an A for the offseason and a D for making us watch Behren Morton.

items.headline: Patriots scouting turnover continues with Robert Haines hire from Carolina
items.snark: More front-office musical chairs. At least this time they hired a guy with 22 years of experience.

items.headline: Patriots free agents Dre'Mont Jones, Alijah Vera-Tucker hungry for playoffs
items.snark: Jones and Vera-Tucker signed with New England to finally see January football. Bold strategy, fellas.

items.headline: Mike Giardi: Super Bowl loss lingers and that may be good for Patriots
items.snark: Losing the Super Bowl still stings and that's actually great motivation, sure, whatever helps you sleep.
